fields.name: apply_text_document_edit
documentation: Applies a `TextDocumentEdit`, which is a list of changes to a single
@*param* `index` — : Optional index of the edit, if from a list of edits (or nil, if not from a list)

fields.name: apply_text_edits
documentation: function M.apply_text_edits(text_edits: lsp.TextEdit[], bufnr: integer, position_encoding: 'utf-16'|'utf-32'|'utf-8')
Applies a list of text edits to a buffer.

fields.name: buf_clear_references
documentation: Removes document highlights from a buffer.

fields.name: buf_highlight_references
documentation: function M.buf_highlight_references(bufnr: integer, references: lsp.DocumentHighlight[], position_encoding: 'utf-16'|'utf-32'|'utf-8')
Shows a list of document highlights for a certain buffer.

fields.name: character_offset
documentation: function M.character_offset(buf: integer, row: integer, col: integer, offset_encoding?: 'utf-16'|'utf-32'|'utf-8')
Returns the UTF-32 and UTF-16 offsets for a position in a certain buffer.
defaults to `offset_encoding` of first client of `buf`
@*return* — `offset_encoding` index of the character in line {row} column {col} in buffer {buf}

fields.name: convert_input_to_markdown_lines
documentation: a list of lines containing valid markdown. Useful to populate the hover
window for `textDocument/hover`, for parsing the result of
Note that if the input is of type `MarkupContent` and its kind is `plaintext`,
then the corresponding value is returned without further modifications.
@*param* `contents` — List of strings to extend with converted lines. Defaults to {}.

fields.name: convert_signature_help_to_markdown_lines
documentation: function M.convert_signature_help_to_markdown_lines(signature_help: lsp.SignatureHelp, ft?: string, triggers?: string[])
@*param* `ft` — filetype that will be use as the `lang` for the label markdown code block
@*param* `triggers` — list of trigger characters from the lsp server. used to better determine parameter offsets
@*return* — highlight range for the active parameter

fields.name: jump_to_location
documentation: function M.jump_to_location(location: lsp.Location|lsp.LocationLink, position_encoding?: 'utf-16'|'utf-32'|'utf-8', reuse_win?: boolean)
@*param* `reuse_win` — Jump to existing window if buffer is already open.

fields.name: locations_to_items
documentation: Returns the items with the byte position calculated correctly and in sorted
order, for display in quickfix and location lists.
The `user_data` field of each resulting item will contain the original
The result can be passed to the {list} argument of |setqflist()| or
default to first client of buffer

fields.name: lookup_section
documentation: Helper function to return nested values in language server settings
@*param* `section` — indicating the field of the settings table
@*return* `The` — value of settings accessed via section. `vim.NIL` if not found.

fields.name: make_floating_popup_options

Creates a table with sensible default options for a floating window. The

fields.name: make_formatting_params

Defaults to the start of the last visual selection.
Defaults to the end of the last visual selection.

fields.name: make_position_params
documentation: Creates a `TextDocumentPositionParams` object for the current buffer and cursor position.

fields.name: make_range_params
documentation: Using the current position in the current buffer, creates an object that
can be used as a building block for several LSP requests, such as
@*param* `window` — : |window-ID| or 0 for current, defaults to current

fields.name: make_text_document_params
documentation: Creates a `TextDocumentIdentifier` object for the current buffer.

fields.name: open_floating_preview
documentation: function M.open_floating_preview(contents: table, syntax: string, opts?: vim.lsp.util.open_floating_preview.Opts)
Shows contents in a floating window.
@*param* `contents` — of lines to show in window
@*param* `syntax` — of syntax to set for opened buffer
before they are passed on to |nvim_open_win()|)
@*return* `winid` — of newly created float window preview window

fields.name: preview_location
documentation: Previews a location in a floating window
behavior depends on type of location:
- for Location, range is shown (e.g., function definition)
- for LocationLink, targetRange is shown (e.g., body of function definition)

fields.name: rename
documentation: function M.rename(old_fname: string, new_fname: string, opts?: vim.lsp.util.rename.Opts)
Existing buffers are renamed as well, while maintaining their bufnr.
It deletes existing buffers that conflict with the renamed file name only when
* the conflicting buffers are not loaded, so that deleting them does not result in data loss.

fields.name: set_lines
documentation: function M.set_lines(lines: string[], A: [integer, integer], B: [integer, integer], new_lines: string[])
Replaces text in a range with new text.
@*param* `new_lines` — list of strings to replace the original

fields.name: show_document
documentation: function M.show_document(location: lsp.Location|lsp.LocationLink, position_encoding?: 'utf-16'|'utf-32'|'utf-8', opts?: vim.lsp.util.show_document.Opts)
Shows document and optionally jumps to the location.

fields.name: stylize_markdown
documentation: function M.stylize_markdown(bufnr: integer, contents: string[], opts?: table)
Converts markdown into syntax highlighted regions by stripping the code
blocks and converting them into highlighted code.
This will by default insert a blank line separator after those code block
This method configures the given buffer and returns the lines to set.
If you want to open a popup with fancy markdown, use `open_floating_preview` instead
@*param* `contents` — of lines to show in window
- wrap_at   character to wrap at for computing height
- max_width  maximal width of floating window
- max_height maximal height of floating window
- separator insert separator after code block

fields.name: symbols_to_items
documentation: Converts symbols to quickfix list items.
@*param* `bufnr` — buffer handle or 0 for current, defaults to current
default to first client of buffer

fields.name: trim_empty_lines
documentation: Removes empty lines from the beginning and end.

fields.name: try_trim_markdown_code_blocks
documentation: Accepts markdown lines and tries to reduce them to a filetype if they
comprise just a single code block.
